title: SQL Injection via Untrusted Input
description: Attackers inject malicious SQL code into input fields (e.g., login forms, search boxes) to manipulate database queries, potentially gaining unauthorized access to sensitive data or modifying system functionality.
attack_vector: Unvalidated user input directly used in SQL queries. Lack of proper input sanitization or parameterized queries.
mitigations: Implement parameterized queries or prepared statements to prevent SQL injection.
Input validation and sanitization to filter out malicious characters and patterns.
Least privilege principle – restrict database user permissions to the minimum required.
